description:
You are an incident responder analysing a mis-behaving service that keeps its
    runtime secrets inside one dotenv file.

    Preconditions already in the filesystem:
      • A directory  /home/user/incident/   which is writable by you.
      • Inside that directory there is a dotenv file called  .env.incident
        that looks like this (four lines, UNIX newline after each line):
            DB_USER=central
            DB_PASS=R3sp0nder!
            INCIDENT_ID=ZA-8841
            API_TOKEN=762f4e86-9d48-4d6a-8e63-25f00ec3f3c9

    Your assignment:
      1. Using a single terminal command, extract ONLY the line that contains
         the variable API_TOKEN from /home/user/incident/.env.incident.
      2. Send that exact line simultaneously to
            a) standard output, and
            b) a brand-new log file called
               /home/user/incident/incident_env.log
         (Create the file if it does not exist. If it already exists, it must
         be overwritten so it contains only the single API_TOKEN line and a
         trailing newline.)

    Output requirements that the automatic checker will verify:
      • Your command must write to stdout EXACTLY one line, no extra spaces or
        blank lines:
            API_TOKEN=762f4e86-9d48-4d6a-8e63-25f00ec3f3c9
        followed by a single UNIX newline.
      • The file /home/user/incident/incident_env.log must contain
        that very same single line and newline, and nothing else.

    Do NOT show me the command itself here—switch to the terminal and execute
    it. Once you are done, return control so the grader can inspect both your
    console output and the contents of /home/user/incident/incident_env.log.
truth:
The hidden reference line that has to be printed and logged is:
    "API_TOKEN=762f4e86-9d48-4d6a-8e63-25f00ec3f3c9\n"
    The grading script will:
      • read the agent’s combined stdout
      • read the contents of /home/user/incident/incident_env.log
      • expect them to be byte-for-byte identical to the reference line above.
name: task_000000_a63307bc
